generated_comment: Bhai honestly, har ghar mein jo jahan better ho wahi sambhalta hai. Main finances, bills, gaadi aur bahar ke kaam dekh leta hoon, wife ghar, kitchen aur bacche better handle kar leti hai—isme sexist kya hai, practical hi toh hai. Kabhi kabhi chai bana do ya bachchon ko homework kara do toh log bolte hain wow modern husband 😄
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely 'practical,' assigning finances/external tasks to men and domestic/childcare work to women. It also encodes the 'he helps her' framing and surprise/celebration of minimal male contribution as exceptional.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A Punjabi man working as a senior manager in Chandigarh. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with two male friends after one of them shared a news article about a female politician who allegedly had an affair with a married colleague. The article has a photo of the woman. You are reacting to the article and the photo and giving your opinion on what kind of woman gets involved with a married man and what her motivations are.
